message:
So last month my kid burned toast. Not a disaster—just *dark* toast. But the smoke alarm went off, and suddenly the family group chat absolutely *exploded*. 'IS THAT OUR HOUSE?' 'MOM ARE YOU OK??' 'CALL 911' 😅

Turns out my partner had never actually *heard* our smoke alarm before. My parents didn't know where our fire extinguisher was. And nobody—and I mean nobody—could tell me our actual escape route without me walking them through it.

It was such a small moment, but it made us realize we'd never actually *talked* about this stuff together. So we spent a lazy Sunday doing exactly that: walking through the house, testing alarms, pointing at windows, talking about the plan. Made it pretty simple and real, not scary.

Honestly? Best use of screen time that week. 😊

Does your family actually know where to go if the alarm goes off? This little refresher got us all on the same page.
